name: Zerodha-Style Real-Time Market Data Processor
scenario: Build a real-time market data processing engine. Price ticks arrive continuously (produced into a BlockingQueue). Multiple worker threads consume ticks and update per-symbol statistics (ConcurrentHashMap + AtomicLong). A reporter thread reads statistics and prints a live dashboard. All concurrent, all lock-free or fine-grained.
description: A real-time tick processor demonstrating all concurrent collection types: BlockingQueue for tick stream, ConcurrentHashMap for per-symbol stats, AtomicInteger for tick counts, and CopyOnWriteArrayList for alert subscriptions.

core.description: `TickProducer` — generates 200 random ticks for 5 symbols into a `LinkedBlockingQueue<PriceTick>(100)`. Runs as a daemon thread.

`MarketStats` class using `ConcurrentHashMap<String, AtomicLong>` for tick counts, `ConcurrentHashMap<String, AtomicReference<Double>>` for last price. Methods: recordTick(PriceTick), getTickCount(symbol), getLastPrice(symbol).
3 `TickConsumer` worker threads consuming from the BlockingQueue, calling marketStats.recordTick() for each tick. Use poll(200ms) with null check for graceful shutdown.
After all ticks are produced and consumed: print final stats per symbol (tick count, last price) sorted by tick count descending.
Global AtomicLong totalTicks — incremented for every tick processed. Final value should exactly equal 200.
Proper shutdown: producer finishes → workers drain queue → all join → print report.
Zero race conditions: all shared state accessed through concurrent types only.

bonus.description: Add a `PriceAlertSystem` using `CopyOnWriteArrayList<PriceAlert>`: add/remove alerts, and after each tick check all alerts. Alert fires if price crosses threshold. Show that alert iteration is safe while ticks are processed concurrently.

Add VWAP (Volume Weighted Average Price) per symbol: `ConcurrentHashMap<String, AtomicLong>` for totalValue (price * volume as long cents) and totalVolume. Print VWAP = totalValue / totalVolume for each symbol.
Add high-water mark tracking using AtomicReference<Double> and compareAndSet: if new price > current high, atomically update. Print per-symbol high-water mark at the end.

data_to_use:
Symbols: RELIANCE, TCS, INFY, HDFC, WIPRO
Ticks: 200 random ticks, ~40 per symbol on average
Price range: symbol base ± 2% random walk
Volume: random 100-10000 per tick
example_output:
=== Market Data Processor ===
Starting 1 producer + 3 consumers...
[processing 200 ticks...]

=== Final Statistics ===
Symbol       Ticks  Last Price
-----------------------------
RELIANCE       43   ₹2462.50
TCS            41   ₹3891.20
INFY           40   ₹1558.30
WIPRO          39   ₹413.70
HDFC           37   ₹1679.40

Total ticks processed: 200
Queue empty: true

criteria: poll() with timeout for graceful shutdown (10 pts)
All threads join before report (5 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key design: producer is daemon (JVM won't wait for it). Workers use poll(200ms) — returns null after 200ms with no tick, then check if producer is done AND queue is empty → stop. AtomicReference<Double> with compareAndSet for high-water mark (bonus). MarketStats is completely lock-free — ConcurrentHashMap handles map-level safety, AtomicLong handles count-level, AtomicReference handles reference-level.
submission_instructions: Save as MarketProcessor.java. Push to GitHub. This demonstrates all four concurrent collection types in one real-time system.
tips_for_success: Producer: runs in daemon thread, loops 200 times putting to queue
Consumer: while(!done || !queue.isEmpty()) { tick = queue.poll(200, ms); if (tick != null) process; }
Last price: computeIfAbsent(sym, k -> new AtomicReference<>(0.0)).set(price)
High-water mark CAS: ref.updateAndGet(old -> Math.max(old, newPrice))
concepts_reinforced: Poll with timeout for graceful worker shutdown
